<!-- Generated by spec-lite | skill: feature | date: 2026-06-09 -->

# Feature: Move & Save Request Dialog Overhaul

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-004
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

Make the move/save request dialog (`RequestSaveWizard`) trustworthy and unambiguous. Today users can't tell where a request currently lives (the dialog shows only the folder path, never the collection name), the destination-folder dropdown is mislabeled "(optional)" and vanishes entirely unless the typed query exactly matches a collection with folders, folder names containing `/` corrupt the selected path, and moving onto an existing same-named request silently overwrites it. After this feature: move mode always shows `Current location: <Collection> / <folders>`, a `Destination Folder` selector is always visible for a valid destination, option values are delimiter-safe, and same-name conflicts at the destination error instead of overwriting.

**Verified issues** (all confirmed in [RequestSaveWizard.tsx](../../packages/core/src/components/common/RequestSaveWizard.tsx) + [CollectionsPane.tsx](../../packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionsPane.tsx)):
- Current path line (lines 185-189) renders only `currentPath.join(' / ')` or `(Root)` — no collection name; the wizard never receives `sourceCollectionName` (it exists in `MoveRequestDraft` but isn't passed).
- Folder selector (line 230) gated by `isExistingCollection && folderPathOptions.length > 0`; `folderPathOptions` requires `searchQuery` to exactly equal a collection name (lines 61-71) — mid-typing or create-new toggling hides it; a collection with no folders never shows even `(Root)`.
- Option values round-trip through `opt.path.join('/')` / `value.split('/')` (lines 240-246) — folder names containing `/` corrupt the path.
- `handleConfirmMoveRequest` ([CollectionsPane.tsx:445-525](../../packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionsPane.tsx)) save-then-delete is correct, but the service's name-match overwrite means a same-named request at the destination is silently replaced.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No persisted-data changes. Component contract changes only:

### Entities & Attributes

- **RequestSaveWizardProps** (extended):
  - `sourceCollectionName` (string, optional) — NEW; collection display name of the request's current home (move mode)
  - `currentPath` (string[], optional) — existing; source folder path (move mode); also still used as destination prefill
  - existing: `isOpen`, `onClose`, `onSave(collectionName, requestName, folderPath)`, `mode`, `initialCollectionName`, `initialRequestName`
- **Folder option value encoding** (internal): options keyed by **index** into the `FolderPathOption[]` array (`value: String(index)`); the selected `path: string[]` is looked up by index — never serialized through a joined string. `(Root)` is index 0 (the `getFolderPathOptions` root entry).

### Relationships

- `CollectionsPane.MoveRequestDraft` already carries `sourceCollectionName` + `sourceParentPath` → passed through to the wizard.
- Same dialog serves the editor "Save to Collection" flow (both `App.tsx` files) — changes must keep save mode working identically.

### Indexes & Constraints

- Destination conflict rule (move mode): if an item with the same name (case-insensitive) already exists at the destination folder of the destination collection, the move errors — no silent overwrite.

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/components/common/RequestSaveWizard.tsx` — current-location line, folder selector visibility/labels, index-keyed options, new prop
- `packages/core/src/components/common/CollectionsPane.tsx` — pass `sourceCollectionName`; destination same-name conflict check in `handleConfirmMoveRequest`
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/RequestSaveWizard.test.tsx` — component tests (extend or create)
- `docs/features/collections.md` — short note on move semantics (conflict behavior)

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-003** (Collection Operations Integrity): id-retention makes the moved item keep its identity, and the slice re-key changes `updateCollection` callers inside `handleConfirmMoveRequest` (currently keyed by `sourceCollectionName` at lines 514/523 — FEAT-003 TASK-002 converts these to filename). Implement FEAT-003 first; this feature's conflict check assumes the new full-item save contract.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Always-clear current location in move mode

- [x] **Implementation**: Add `sourceCollectionName?: string` to `RequestSaveWizardProps`. In move mode always render the location line (not only when `currentPath` is truthy): `Current location: {sourceCollectionName} / {folder} / …`, and `{sourceCollectionName} (root)` when `currentPath` is empty. In `CollectionsPane.tsx`, pass `sourceCollectionName={moveRequestDraft.sourceCollectionName}` and `currentPath={moveRequestDraft.sourceParentPath}` where the wizard is rendered in move mode.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: RTL — move mode with nested path renders `Current location: My Collection / Folder1 / Sub`; move mode at root renders `My Collection (root)`; save mode renders no location line.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the new prop.
- **Verify**: Open "Move" on a request nested two folders deep — the dialog states collection + full folder path; on a root-level request it states `<Collection> (root)`.

### TASK-002: Destination Folder selector — always visible, clear labels

- [x] **Implementation**: Restructure the folder selector block:
  - Visible whenever a valid destination collection is selected (`isExistingCollection`), **regardless** of `folderPathOptions.length` — a folderless collection shows just `(Root)`. When creating a new collection, show the selector disabled with `(Root)` (a new collection has no folders).
  - Label: `Destination Folder` (both modes — drop "Move to Folder (optional)"/"Save to Folder (optional)"). Helper text: `Pick a folder or keep (Root)`.
  - Default selection `(Root)`; reset to `(Root)` when the destination collection changes (existing reset logic stays).
  - `getFolderPathOptions` already returns the full tree of the selected collection including the root entry — the gating logic was the bug; no changes to the util.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: RTL — selecting a collection with no folders still shows the selector with `(Root)`; selecting a collection with nested folders lists all depths (`Folder1`, `Folder1 / Sub`); switching collections resets selection to `(Root)`; "create new collection" shows disabled selector at `(Root)`; label and helper text assert the new copy.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: None beyond component JSDoc.
- **Verify**: In the move dialog, pick a *different* collection than the source — its full folder tree is offered immediately, including while the search text was typed via selection (no vanishing dropdown).

### TASK-003: Delimiter-safe folder option values (index-keyed)

- [x] **Implementation**: Replace `value: opt.path.join('/')` / `value.split('/')` with index-keyed options: `options = folderPathOptions.map((opt, i) => ({ label: opt.displayPath, value: String(i) }))`; `setSelectedValue` looks up `folderPathOptions[Number(value)].path`; `selectedValue` derives from the currently selected index (state holds the index or the `path` array with index lookup). No joined-string round-trip anywhere.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: RTL — a folder named `a/b` (containing a slash) is selectable and `onSave` receives `['a/b']` (one segment, not two); nested selection passes the exact `string[]` from `FolderPathOption.path`.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: Inline comment explaining why options are index-keyed (delimiter safety).
- **Verify**: Create a folder literally named `a/b`, move a request into it — it lands in that folder, not in `a` → `b`.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002

### TASK-004: Move conflict check — error instead of silent overwrite

- [x] **Implementation**: In `handleConfirmMoveRequest` (CollectionsPane.tsx), before calling `saveRequestToCollection`: locate the destination collection in the store (existing lookup), walk `destinationFolderPath` to the target folder's items, and if an item with the same trimmed name (case-insensitive) and a **different `id`** than the moved item exists, show `notification.showNotification('error', …)` naming the conflict and abort (dialog stays open via no-op). Same-id match (moving back onto itself in another folder scenario) is allowed — it's an update, not an overwrite of a different request.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: handler-level tests (or RTL through the pane) — move onto a same-named different-id request errors and performs no adapter calls; move to a clean folder proceeds (save then delete order asserted); same-collection same-path move still short-circuits with the existing error.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: `docs/features/collections.md` — one line: moving a request errors if the destination already has an item with that name.
- **Verify**: Create requests named `X` in two collections; move one onto the other's folder — error notification, both originals intact on disk.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001 (uses the same draft data), FEAT-003 TASK-003

### TASK-005: Save-flow parity verification (editor "Save to Collection")

- [x] **Implementation**: The editor save flow (both `App.tsx` files → `RequestSaveWizard` in `save` mode) shares the fixed component — verify it now exposes the full folder tree for any selected collection and that saving into an existing sub-folder works from this entry point; fix any save-mode-specific gating remnants found. No new behavior — this is the parity pass the plan calls out.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: RTL in save mode — request-name field present, folder tree listed for the chosen collection, `onSave` receives `(collectionName, requestName, folderPath)` with a nested path.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: None (covered by TASK-002 copy).
- **Verify**: From the request editor, Save → pick an existing collection → choose a depth-2 folder → request appears in that folder in the tree and on disk.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002, TASK-003

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: All failures surface via `notification.showNotification('error', …)` per memory; adapter calls already return `Result` and are checked (`saveResult.isOk` / `deleteResult.isOk` paths unchanged). The conflict check happens client-side pre-save; FEAT-003's service-side validations remain the backstop.
- **Logging**: N/A beyond FEAT-003's mutation-failure logging (this feature adds no new service paths).
